title: Lesson 6 Overview
subtitle: Listen to the lesson explained in English

transcript.en: Hey! 👋 Lesson 6 — last lesson of Module 2!

Four things: **dining table** vocab, **traditional Maltese food**, the **question words** like who and where, and one tricky present-tense pattern: **verbs with the letter għ**. Three minutes. Let's go!
All the things you'd find at a Maltese dinner. Let's run through it:
🚨 Heads up — **the plurals are wildly irregular**. Don't expect a tidy '+s'.
Yeah. Wild. The cards walk you through every single one. 📚
Now the fun part. **Maltese classics**. Some of these you'll have eaten without knowing the name. 😋
The flaky savoury pastry — pea or cheese:
Bread with oil and tomato — basically the perfect snack:
Twelve dishes total. Reads more like a menu than a vocab list. 🍴
**Eight words. They unlock most everyday conversation.**
Like in English, they slot in at the start of a question. The lesson has a **short story** about Mark and Maria playing football — and questions practising every one of these. 🎯
OK this one's the trickiest of the lesson. Take a breath. ✋
Some Maltese verbs contain the letter **għ** — the silent-but-stretchy one. The rules for conjugating these into the present tense follow **two model verbs**.
MODEL 1 — **Rifes**. For verbs that **start** with għ. Like:
Notice the **prefix slips before the għ**. ✨
MODEL 2 — **seRaq**. For verbs where għ sits **in the middle**. Like:
Don't worry if this feels dense — the **conjugation tables** in the section show every form, and the exercises drill it gently. 🎯
Six lessons. Two modules. The whole course.
From here you can revisit any lesson, drill any section, or just listen to these overviews while you commute. **The work compounds.** 📈
